title: Opendoor Technologies Q1 Earnings: Better-Than-Expected Results
text: Opendoor Technologies exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q1, reporting revenue of $720 million against analyst estimates of $665.2 million. The company's adjusted EBITDA came in at -$31 million, slightly better than the projected -$33.5 million. Key Profitability Achievement: The most significant announcement involved a major operational milestone. According to the company, it is adjusted EBITDA profitable on a 12-month go-forward basis as of April 1, marking a turning point in the firm's path toward sustained profitability. Mixed Q2 Guidance: Management projected more cautious results ahead, guiding for approximately $900 million in sales for Q2 (below analyst expectations of $1.13 billion) with adjusted EBITDA expected to remain roughly flat. Strategic Shifts: Under new leadership, Opendoor has intensified its home-flipping operations and adopted a default to AI approach, incorporating artificial intelligence for property assessments and closing procedures. CEO Kaz Nejatian highlighted the strength of recent cohorts.
